# Daily Notes {.unnumbered}

## 2026-03-19

Reading @Kee2017, chapter 11 for preparing future development of `majordome_engineering.symbolic`; revisited basic definitions of transport coefficients (viscosity, thermal conductivity, diffusivity) based on classical macroscopic *gradient-based* semi-empirical laws.

$$
f = -\mu\frac{du}{dz} \quad\text{,}\quad
q = -\lambda\frac{dT}{dz} \quad\text{,}\quad
J^{\ast} = -D\frac{dC}{dz}
$$

*Mixture-averaged* properties are not accurate and are correct asymptotically only in some special cases; they also fail to preserve mass-conservation in species equations and require corrective measures. On the other hand, multicomponent properties require solving linear systems and are general.
